summary: Platform with multi-entity authentication system supporting separate login pages and dashboards for Hotels, NGOs, Volunteers, and Admins.
architectural_notes: Multi-entity auth: separate login pages and roles for Hotel, NGO, Volunteer, and Admin

existing_features.title: Create a unified login landing page at `/login` that displays four distinct login entry points: Hotel, NGO, Volunteer, and Admin. Each entry point should visually represent the entity with an icon and label, and navigate to that entity's dedicated login page on click.

Create a dedicated Hotel login page at `/login/hotel`. It should use Internet Identity for authentication, set the user's entityType to `hotel` upon first login, and redirect to a Hotel-specific dashboard after successful login.

Create a dedicated NGO login page at `/login/ngo`. It should use Internet Identity for authentication, set the user's entityType to `ngo` upon first login, and redirect to the existing NGO dashboard after successful login.

Create a dedicated Volunteer login page at `/login/volunteer`. It should use Internet Identity for authentication, set the user's entityType to `volunteer` upon first login, and redirect to the existing Volunteer dashboard after successful login.

Create a dedicated Admin login page at `/login/admin`. It should use Internet Identity for authentication, set the user's entityType to `admin` upon first login, and redirect to the existing Admin Analytics dashboard after successful login.

Create a Hotel-specific dashboard page at `/hotel-dashboard`. It should display hotel-relevant actions such as posting surplus food donations and viewing donation history. Protect this route so only users with entityType `hotel` can access it; others should be redirected to `/login/hotel`.

Update the `RoleGuard` component and the `useRoleBasedAccess` hook to handle the new `hotel` entity type in addition to the existing roles, granting hotel users access to `/hotel-dashboard` and the food donation form.

Update the main navigation bar in `App.tsx` to show entity-appropriate navigation links based on the logged-in user's entityType. Hotels see donation-related links; NGOs see NGO dashboard; Volunteers see volunteer dashboard; Admins see admin analytics. Unauthenticated users see a link to `/login`.

new_feature_requests.summary: Create separate login pages and authentication flows for Hotel, NGO, Volunteer, and Admin entities

Extend the backend data model to support entity-type-specific user registration and authentication. Add an `entityType` field (variant: #hotel, #ngo, #volunteer, #admin) to the UserProfile type. Provide backend endpoints to register and retrieve profiles by entity type, and a dedicated admin role check for protected operations.
